title: Dandruff follow-up
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

presenting_history.text: Supports seborrheic-dermatitis and dandruff terminology, scalp or facial scale, pruritus, greasy plaques, erythema, scalp, eyebrow, nasolabial, eyelid, ear, trunk, fold, relapsing, stress, and seasonal context.
presenting_history.rationale: The UAE DHA seborrheic-dermatitis guideline supports documenting scalp scale, itch, distribution, duration, recurrence, triggers, impact, morphology, and change when dandruff is clinically considered within that spectrum.
presenting_history.evidence_extract: Supports seborrheic-dermatitis and dandruff terminology, scalp or facial scale, pruritus, greasy plaques, erythema, scalp, eyebrow, nasolabial, eyelid, ear, trunk, fold, relapsing, stress, and seasonal context.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Seborrheic Dermatitis

exact_section.heading: Sections 1 and 5 — background, clinical history, distribution, course, and diagnosis

red_flags.text: Supports fever or systemic illness, painful rapidly worsening eczema, recurrent or spreading infection, and clinician-documented worsening or return fields.
red_flags.rationale: The red-flag section supports recording severe, widespread, infected, immunocompromised, or uncertain presentations only when assessed, plus clinician decisions.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports fever or systemic illness, painful rapidly worsening eczema, recurrent or spreading infection, and clinician-documented worsening or return fields.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Seborrheic Dermatitis

source_limitations: Scalp and other sites, morphology, itch, oiliness, triggers, prior products, response and adverse effects, differential, diagnosis, treatment, referral, and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
